entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 7 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 7 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-07 04:07AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Chilling moment home in Sydney's west is set alight
  • ‘It’s been intense’: Crown CEO exits after Blackstone takeover
  • Two men arrested over alleged gun supply syndicate
  • Three teens found not guilty of killing Gold Coast teenager Jack Beasley
  • Bernard Collaery’s leaking charges over East Timor operation dropped on Mark Dreyfus’ orders

  • Millions of Australians now eligible for fourth COVID-19 vaccine
  • Michael Daher speaks to investors
  • The deep family connection that drives rapper Tasman Keith
  • Tomljanovic angry with Kyrgios question after Wimbledon quarter-final loss
  • 9 to 5 the Musical was in trouble. Time to ‘harness the power of Dolly’
  • ‘Not fair to Zoe’: Teals back away from state push in Brighton, Caulfield, Sandringham
  • Tupou back for Wallabies but Vunivalu debut will have to wait
  • De Goey’s quad to be assessed, Moore ruled out; McEvoy back for Hawks after serious neck injury
  • Delivery driver’s face slashed when he tried to deliver pizza

  • John Barilaro appointed to US trade role over shortlisted applicant Kimberley Cole, documents show

  • ‘The world is looking at Beijing’: Wong calls on China to try to stop Putin’s war

  • We can’t buy back every home on a floodplain, but we can stop building for certain disaster
  • Sydney's week of wild weather

  • Embattled Boris Johnson has one obvious choice to make

  • US beachgoers confront man sitting in turtle nesting area

  • Sex trafficking can justify homicide, US court rules

  • UK PM’s leaderships hands in the balance after senior ministers quit

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 7 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Accessing an abortion in Australia just got a bit easier
  • The cringe of Boris Johnson
  • Boris Johnson defies calls to resign amid mass exodus from his government
  • Iran says former British diplomat among several foreigners detained
  • Boris Johnson is under pressure to resign. How did he get to this point?
  • Bishops suspend priest convicted of child sex abuse, reopen probe
  • A rescue mission outside of Kyiv
  • Russia’s offensive gains pace in Donetsk; focus shifts to Slovyansk
  • FBI director suggests China bracing for sanctions if it invades Taiwan

  • Pope announces plans to expand women’s role in Vatican
  • Wealthy Mideast nations boost spending to protect citizens from inflation
  • Central America hoped bitcoin would attract tourists. It hasn’t worked.
  • I covered Somalia’s last famine a decade ago. It’s about to happen again.
  • #GentleMinions trend of suited-up teens irks — and amuses — moviegoers
  • NATO signs accession protocols for Finland and Sweden as Ukraine faces shelling in east
  • Tuna catch dries up for Kenya's local fisherfolk
  • Hurricane Bonnie weakens as it heads out over open Pacific
  • Children among 50 held 100 days after Nigeria train attack

  • Britain's Boris Johnson battles to stay as PM amid revolt
  • UN: 2.3 billion people severely or moderately hungry in 2021
  • Iranian TV: Revolutionary Guard accuses diplomats of spying
  • With little outcry, Chicago’s bloody weekend eclipsed Highland Park toll
  • Uvalde officer spotted gunman, then asked for permission to shoot, report says
  • How the Highland Park parade shooting unfolded: A marching band, then gunshots
  • Highland Park shooting suspect held without bond; past incidents raise questions
  • Suspect confessed to mass shooting, considered 2nd attack, authorities say
  • Trying to stay safe in a mass shooting, and to overcome the fear they create
  • Mississippi’s last abortion clinic will shutter as trigger ban begins
  • Man who punched 5-year-old and father charged with hate crime, police say
  • Justice Dept. operation targeting violent crime nets 1,500 arrests
  • Hogan orders relaxed rules for Maryland concealed handgun permits
  • Experts hope LGBTQ youth will call 988 — a new suicide lifeline number
  • Highland Park suspect’s father sponsored gun permit application, police say
  • As a soldier, he escaped Nazi captors. At 97, he finally got his medals.
  • Biden planned to nominate antiabortion conservative before Roe overturned, emails show
  • Post Politics Now: Biden touts work on bolstering retirement savings in Ohio visit
  • Biden offers midterm preview in Ohio campaign-style visit
  • Former White House counsel Cipollone to testify before Jan. 6 committee
  • Casualties from war in Ukraine include millions of the world’s poor
  • Labor market remains red hot amid growing recession fears
  • Hackers claim they breached data on 1 billion Chinese citizens
  • To get banned abortion pills, patients turn to legally risky tactics
  • Top business executives have gotten even more Republican, study finds
  • Crypto broker Voyager Digital files for bankruptcy as industry falters
  • Apple unveils new security feature to block government spyware
  • Dreading the knock brush before bed? Here’s how to make Slack less chaotic.
  • Distinguished homes for sale in the D.C. region
  • With an abundance of trees, Potomac Woods lives up to its name
  • Buyers failed the good faith test, and sellers contemplate next steps
  • What to look out for when signing a lease
  • At 5 Years, India’s Historic Tax Reform Is Out of Fuel
  • Bond Traders Are as Confused as Anyone These Days
  • Indonesia’s Shuttle Diplomacy Can Be More Than Empty Miles
  • Who Would Replace Boris Johnson? Here Are His Likely Successors
  • How Inflation Fears Drove a Historic Global Bond Rout
  • What’s Crypto Lending, and Why Did Investors Get Burned?
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • A ‘Price Cap’ on Russian Energy — Could That Work?
  • Dear President Biden: Please don’t break your promise to shun Saudi Arabia
  • Busting the filibuster for abortion now is madness
  • Police kill another Black man — and the same old questions arise
  • Give Biden a break
  • A daring flight to safety — and a story Biden should tell MBS
  • Please, Republicans. Don’t nominate Kari Lake for Arizona governor.
  • The proposed new names for military bases are in, and they’re inspiring
  • Democratic alarms sound over DeSantis’s new elections overseer
  • Why the Buffalo supermarket slaughter should be a death penalty case
  • Give Ukraine more artillery, and let the Navy break Russia’s blockade
  • DeSantis is smarter than Trump. That may make him more of a threat.
  • A debt crisis in Sri Lanka could spread worldwide. The U.S. must help.
  • Youngkin’s pragmatic school board picks offer hope for reform
  • Democrats should listen to Kamala Harris and follow her example.
  • Is Joe Manchin about to do the right thing?
  • Why are we accepting the mass slaughter of animals through heatstroke?

  • Rights aren’t negated by other rights
  • Youngkin should stay clear of the process

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 7 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 7 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-07 03:29AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong power company rules out foul play in cable-bridge fire that caused blackouts across 175,000 households and shops
  • Rents near Hong Kong’s universities on the rise as more mainland Chinese students return amid easing coronavirus crisis
  • ‘We have to tell people how great we are’: Hong Kong’s John Lee to adopt more proactive approach to promoting city on world stage
  • American businesses in Hong Kong face operational risks similar to those in mainland China: US envoy
  • Hong Kong-mainland border reopening: what if Covid-19 containment fails?
  • US targets Hong Kong and UAE companies in fresh Iran sanctions
  • Civil service unions demand meeting with new Hong Kong leader John Lee over ‘insulting’ 2.5 per cent pay rise offer
  • Hong Kong doctors issue urgent appeal for lung donation to save woman’s life
  • Beijing’s top diplomat in Hong Kong urges foreign consulates to present city in ‘more objective manner’ at briefing
  • Owners of Hong Kong sawmill appeal for help to move operations after being told to make way for public housing
  • New Hong Kong leader John Lee to set up 4 task forces on key livelihood issues, tells ministers to come up with policy targets by end of August
  • Hong Kong Book Fair organiser dismisses suggestions of censorship despite some publishers being banned from event
  • It’s time to accept Basic Law, former Hong Kong leader CY Leung tells opposition camp, supporters
  • Coronavirus: charges dropped against 3 chiefs of Hong Kong Scout Association accused of hosting banquet that violated social-distancing curbs
  • Tianqi Lithium to offer Hong Kong IPO at top of price range, as improving sentiment offers hope for companies awaiting the ‘right moment’
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong will not return to toughest control rules for arrivals, John Lee says, as health officials warn of rising trend in severe cases
  • How Communist Party membership and state-sector jobs became fashionable choices for young Chinese
  • Nasa’s Capstone satellite phones home and is on course for the moon
  • Wang Yi’s Myanmar trip could derail Asean’s peace plan by legitimising junta, analysts say
  • China robotics industry cools down as top maker of robot waiting staff slashes jobs
  • Chinese team develops world’s most sensitive wind-detecting laser
  • Chinese smartphone maker Xiaomi taps Vietnam as production base for Southeast Asia market amid supply chain disruptions at home
  • Despite end of Meng Wanzhou and ‘two Michaels’ crises, chill lingers over Canada-China relations
  • US-China relations: Beijing could face double threat from tariffs and Indo-Pacific pact
  • Directors of FBI and Britain’s MI5 raise fresh alarms about Chinese spying in rare joint speech
  • China’s flagging birth rate, weak consumer spending prompt top online baby-and-mom emporium Mia.com to trim operations
  • China Evergrande launches first EV, the Hengchi 5, at just over half the price of Tesla’s Model Y as embattled developer aims to revive fortunes
  • China-US ties: foreign ministers to canvas friction points on G20 sidelines but no breakthrough expected
  • China ready for ‘golden era’ with the Philippines under Marcos Jnr
  • Chinese astronomy satellite discovers strongest magnetic field found in the universe so far
  • Beijing brings in vaccine mandate as city reports cases of new Omicron subvariant
  • China’s all-purpose WeChat app lets users register two accounts with one phone number in pilot test
  • Chinese pork stocks including WH Group recede as nation’s top planning body cracks down on misconduct, ‘vile speculation’ stoking hog prices
  • Alibaba’s home city removes name of Chinese e-commerce giant from future metro map
  • Tencent to close Steam-like mobile game portal as market prospects dim under tight regulation
  • Russian warships sailing near Taiwan puts Beijing in ‘an awkward position’, say analysts
  • China delivers US$37 million in aid to Afghanistan, fulfilling promise to Taliban
  • US pushes Netherlands to ban Dutch tech supplier ASML from selling older semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China
  • Belarus’s JD.com and Douyin online stores woo Chinese consumers with chocolates and crisps
  • Angry Australians feel Qantas is hardly reliable as flight frustrations boil over
  • Singapore confirms first local monkeypox case
  • As China and Russia champion an expanded BRICS, India may have to pick a side
  • Prison and tougher penalties for cyberbullying in Japan after tragic death of Netflix star Hana Kimura
  • Coronavirus: new cases top 45,000 in Japan, nearly double last week’s figure
  • Australia will avoid engaging Asean through China ‘prism’, Foreign Minister Penny Wong says
  • Singapore hangs two more men amid continued outcry over capital punishment
  • Global food fears ease as price of cooking oil and grains plummets
  • ‘Ferdinand Marcos Airport’ proposal by Philippine President Marcos Jnr’s ally draws sharp criticism
  • Japanese grandpa turns accidental YouTube star with ‘warm and peaceful’ art tutorials
  • Suspected jihadists storm Nigeria prison, freeing hundreds
  • US July 4 parade gunman considered second attack while on the run
  • Boris Johnson fires Michael Gove, defies cabinet colleagues’ calls to step down
  • Iran media says foreign diplomats arrested for alleged spying
  • Covid-19 cases up 30 per cent in 2 weeks, with Europe at centre of new wave, WHO says
  • WHO: More than 6,000 monkeypox cases reported globally, another emergency meeting set
  • Ukrainian children play football in bombed-out stadium amid attempts to regain normality
  • UK PM Boris Johnson vows to ‘keep going’, says he’ll stay in job despite further resignations from his own party
  • Britain’s new finance minister Nadhim Zahawi inherits an economic crisis
  • ‘No safe place’ from artillery as Russians advance in Ukraine’s Donetsk
  • OPEC’S Secretary General Mohammad Barkindo dies at 63
  • UK newspapers: ‘Game over’ for Boris Johnson after key ministers quit
  • Kaisa CEO’s Hong Kong luxury home fetches HK$300 million as Chinese developer sells assets to pare debt
  • Hong Kong stocks skid toward two-week low as Covid-19 warnings undermine recovery optimism
  • China’s first carbon neutrality ETFs raise more than US$1.9 billion as appetite grows for sustainable investing
  • Could ‘productivism’ fill the gap in a polarised era of waning globalisation?
  • Tesla in for further setback after losing EV crown to BYD, as some production is suspended to upgrade Shanghai assembly line
  • Chinese stocks to continue outgunning global peers as UBS wealth managers join bullish camp on second-half outlook
  • China Huadian’s renewable energy unit files for US$4.5 billion Shanghai IPO to fund expansion of solar and wind capacity
  • China is in no danger of losing its supply chain supremacy to India or Vietnam
  • 8 of Hong Kong’s best boutique bean-to-bar chocolate makers, producing single origin bars with eclectic flavours from dried shrimp to strawberry chilli
  • 10 best workation destinations in 2022 – or are they? New list sparks many questions
  • Why dark chocolate, not milk chocolate, is good for you, its health benefits including whether it boosts libido, and what to be careful of
  • World’s 50 Best Restaurants 2022 51-100 list: Hong Kong, Singapore each with 2 new entries; 7 Asian cities make up 14 positions
  • How to avoid childhood obesity in your family: experts say to make sure your kids eat healthy food, exercise and get enough sleep – and lead by example
  • Best Hong Kong restaurants of ex-TVB and CCTV presenter James Chau, from Casa Lisboa to the Ladies Recreation Club
  • How big is too big when it comes to K-pop groups like NCT? With three more singers possibly joining, how far can boy band go?
  • Hong Kong house gets an Asian holiday home vibe, with Hermès-inspired colours and warm wood all over
  • Is missing ‘crypto queen’ Ruja Ignatova the new Anna Sorokin? She’s only the 11th woman to appear on the FBI’s Most Wanted Fugitives list with her OneCoin scam – and a TV series is already in the works
  • Is Damian Lewis dating Alison Mosshart? A year after the death of his wife Helen McCrory, the Homeland actor been hanging out with The Kills singer – so who is she, exactly?
  • Meet Kate Hudson’s multitalented teenage son, Ryder Robinson: the 18 year old just celebrated his one-year anniversary with girlfriend Iris Apatow, and wants to be a rock star like his dad Chris
  • Why is Nick Kyrgios called the bad boy of tennis? Wimbledon’s ‘Wild Thing’ has racked up US$800,000 in fines for bad behaviour ... and was accused of assaulting his ex-girlfriend in Australia
  • Paris Haute Couture Week: Chanel’s autumn/winter 2022-23 collection by Virginie Viard is a dreamy vision of gentle colours, lines and shapes – and statement details
  • Giambattista Valli’s 10th anniversary in haute couture at Paris Fashion Week: the autumn/winter 2022-23 collection was all colourful tulle dresses and shimmering body suits
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 7 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘The world is bloody messy’: Jacinda Ardern urges end to ‘black-and-white’ view of global conflict
  • ‘Get exit done’: UK front pages mix incredulity with PM’s message of defiance
  • Ex-Cheer star Jerry Harris sentenced to 12 years in prison for child sexual abuse images charges
  • New Zealand to embark on world’s largest feral predator eradication

  • US Soccer suspends ex-Toledo coach’s license after Guardian investigation

  • Children too anxious to attend school being failed by English councils – report
  • FBI and MI5 leaders give unprecedented joint warning on Chinese spying
  • Georgia monument dubbed ‘America’s Stonehenge’ damaged by explosives
  • England offer record crowd glimpse of new dawn for women’s football | Jonathan Liew
  • England 1-0 Austria: players ratings from the Euro 2022 opening game | Louise Taylor
  • How much longer can Boris Johnson refuse to budge?
  • Beth Mead edges England over the line against Austria in Euro 2022 opener

  • Boris Johnson admits to private meeting with ex-KGB agent
  • Woman killed as politicians gather on Swedish island of Gotland
  • Nick Kyrgios finds it ‘hard to focus’ on tennis after assault allegation

  • Boris Johnson takes a battering in what may be his last PMQs | John Crace
  • ‘Incredible journey’: Northern Ireland captain Callaghan upbeat for Euro 2022
  • Highland Park shooting: suspect considered attacking another parade
  • New French PM vows to nationalise EDF and tackle cost of living crisis
  • ‘Hangry is a real thing’: psychologists find link between hunger and emotions
  • ‘To my face, nothing’: Poulter breezes into Scottish Open after ban overturned
  • Chelsea close to Raheem Sterling signing after agreeing personal terms
  • Holidaymakers warned of rising coronavirus cases at European destinations
  • Ukraine’s military plans to limit free movement to make conscription easier
  • Zelenskiy calls on trader Vitol to stop shipping Russian ‘blood oil’
  • ‘He would not fit’: Bayern Munich rule out signing Cristiano Ronaldo
  • Everyone can see Johnson has to go, but his party has no grip on reality | David Lammy
  • Two hours of sitting in your car going nowhere: New York’s unique parking rules are back
  • Weekend warriors: why exercise doesn’t have to be regular to be good for you
  • Two bodies found in search following Italian Alps avalanche
  • Tadej Pogacar rides through cobbles and chaos to lay down Tour gauntlet
  • Trump White House counsel to cooperate with January 6 committee
  • UN says nearly 9m people have fled Ukraine since Russian invasion
  • Friends creator: ‘mistake’ to use wrong pronouns for Chandler’s trans parent
  • ‘When we grew up, Luis Buñuel was on Channel 4’: the proudly highbrow Otolith Group
  • Russia-born Rybakina steps up title charge into Wimbledon last four
  • ‘It’s going to be awful’: Tory MPs are gearing up for a vicious succession battle | Katy Balls
  • Keeping mum: TV’s problem with women who don’t want kids
  • Famine: what is it, where will it strike and how should the world respond?
  • Bruno Pereira obituary
  • ‘We didn’t feel it was for us’: the UK’s minority ethnic walking groups tearing down barriers
  • ‘Putin rubbing hands with glee’ after EU votes to class gas and nuclear as green
  • How to make custard tart – recipe | Felicity Cloake's Masterclass
  • As a girl I dreamed about covering women’s football. Now it is a reality | Sophie Downey
  • James Cameron defends three-hour Avatar sequel: ‘I don’t want whining’
  • The revolution will be televised: why we are witnessing a big-boss backlash
  • The US supreme court is turning the constitution into a suicide pact | Lawrence Douglas
  • Girl in the Picture review – the scale of the true-crime monstrosity will leave you reeling
  • Global dismay as supreme court ruling leaves Biden’s climate policy in tatters
  • ‘It shattered my world’: an Ohio soccer coach, sexual misconduct and the system that has protected him
  • ‘First modern novel – oldest language’: Sanskrit translation of Don Quixote rescued from oblivion
  • A moment that changed me: I fled the Gaza blockade to study – but my freedom came at a cost
  • It’s democracy v plutocracy – this is the endgame for our planet | George Monbiot
  • David Hockney: ‘My era was the freest time. I now realise it’s over’
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
